A.reason: Binding a scoped sweep to the branch-advance event genuinely closes the inter-sweep window, but it fires when the daily frame records the advance rather than at the push itself, so it inherits pulse-cadence lag of up to a day.

C.reason: Fires at the exact canon-change commit that stales a waiver — the causing event and the true earliest observable moment a manufactured green becomes false — beating the sweep that would trust the ledger for seven more days.

E.reason: A pre-append gate at write time is zero-lag by construction, and on an append-only spine write time is provably the only moment the failure class (a permanent bad frame) is preventable rather than merely detected.

G.reason: Its firing conditions are already true, which is real (the tear exists now, unpublic, still fixable), but the guard runs at pulse cadence rather than at the handoff event where findings vanish, so it observes the tear up to a pulse late instead of catching it as it happens.

B.reason: Fires at the replication event itself — before divergence is even possible — catching the enabling condition (no canon to arbitrate) rather than any downstream diff, which is pre-causal earliness, not vagueness.

D.reason: Recomputing identity at the rename/transfer event catches detachment at the causing mutation before any mirror caches the stale hash; the fires-on-install clause is legitimate (nulls are genuinely unmeasured) but the daily-sweep leg is ordinary cadence.

F.reason: The chain-link check at emit is zero-lag for a broken spine, but the pin-recompute leg only observes repo divergence at the next pulse — the guard itself concedes it catches pin drift 'one interval old'.

note: The strongest earliest-catch idea in the field is Lane E's pre-append frame gate: on an append-only record, write time is the unique zero-lag moment where prevention exists at all, and a mechanical gate there cannot be beaten by any event- or cadence-triggered detector.
